comment: Side-by-side per register on synthetic_ocr_vi (printed clean/noisy/hard) + brianhuster handwriting. All printed numbers from a single bench_ocr_engines_printed.py run on 2026-05-02 to ensure consistent loader / config across engines. Handwriting numbers from bench_ocr_engines.py.

recommendation.printed_clean_or_noisy: tesseract — 0.00 / 0.70 % CER. EasyOCR (1.42 / 4.87 %) and VietOCR (1.41 / 3.37 %) are tight 2nd; PaddleOCR distant 3rd (24.70 / 31.33 %); RapidOCR unusable.
recommendation.printed_hard_scan: vietocr (slight edge) — 29.00 % vs tesseract 30.34 %; everything else (EasyOCR 87 %, PaddleOCR 86 %, RapidOCR 100 %, TrOCR 94 %) collapses on hard register.
recommendation.handwriting_register: vietocr — 31.82 % CER, 37.5 pp absolute reduction vs tesseract 69.34 %; PaddleOCR 3rd at 59.43 %; RapidOCR effectively non-functional on handwriting line crops (97.20 %).

paddleocr_pp_ocrv5_note: PP-OCRv5 ships no VN-specific recognizer. lang='vi' loads latin_PP-OCRv5_mobile_rec, a generic Latin model that recognizes the alphabet but strips VN tone marks. This is a structural limit, not a config issue — verified by testing default pipeline, doc-prep skipped, standalone TextRecognition, and server-rec model.
rapidocr_note: RapidOCR is the ONNX-runtime port of PaddleOCR. It uses the same recognizer family and exhibits the same diacritic stripping. Docling's ocr_engine='rapidocr' option is therefore not viable for VN.
trocr_handwritten_note: microsoft/trocr-base-handwritten is English-trained; reported here as a baseline reference, not a VN candidate. Random-weight pooler bias warning is expected (encoder.pooler not used during generation).
